posts.text: He’s also a humble, responsible, and kind person and in this hellscape, I think that counts for a lot.

Aaron has been doing this work every day for YEARS-- often unilaterally responsible for driving news cycles and holding elected officials to account for their own words. Show some support if you can!


Aaron Rupar (@atrupar)

If you appreciate my tireless grind to bring you hundreds of newsy clips week in and week out, please support what I do by signing up for my newsletter. I'm really proud of our independent coverage of politics and paid subscribers make my work possible. Thanks. publicnotice

Congratulations to Donald Trump for a truly historic presidency.


Patrick De Haan (@GasBuddyGuy)

BREAKING: For just the second time ever, the U.S. average diesel price has reached $5 per gallon, according to GasBuddy. It's the highest since December 8, 2022 — the only other time diesel crossed $5 nationally.





— https://nitter.net/GasBuddyGuy/status/2033645905585336703#m

He must be Führious about the way things panned out.


Natasha Korecki (@natashakorecki)

CBS: Greg Bovino to retire at the end of the month from federal service. 

What a rapid downfall for an official who the Trump administration granted an immense amount of power in the president's first year in office.  

cbsnews.com/news/gregory-bov…




— https://nitter.net/natashakorecki/status/2033608000758263994#m

It's like an episode of Kids Say The Darndest Things where 5-year-olds are incapable of not blurting out whatever errant thought enters their brain.


Benjamin S. Weiss (@BenjaminSWeiss)

😬Awkward moment at Kennedy Center board meeting when Trump forces Speaker Johnson to talk about FL Rep. Neal Dunn’s illness.

Johnson: “I think it was a terminal diagnosis.”

Trump: “He would be dead by June.”

Johnson: “That wasn’t public, but yeah, okay.”

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/BenjaminSWeiss/sta

No money for healthcare or food stamps or education or infrastructure but unlimited funds for war. Courtesy of the America First administration.


Aaron Rupar (@atrupar)

HASSETT: The latest number I was briefed on is $12 billion

BRENNAN: $12 billion for six weeks of war?

HASSETT: No, I'm sorry. The $12 billion was what I was briefed has been spent so far.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/atrupar/status/2033195807885623395#m

The split screen suggests otherwise


Aaron Rupar (@atrupar)

Whitaker: "President Trump is a diplomacy-first peacemaker"

"Experts say that this is a short term windfall for your ExxonMobils, your Chevrons, your Shells. All oil producers that don’t rely on supplies from the Middle East. Each of those companies gave at least a half million dollars to Trump’s inaugural committee. And oil and gas interests donated $75 million to Trump’s 2024 election campaign.

That’s the quid.
